tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine, proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, and bilirubinuria, with low urine specific gravity and brown, cloudy urine. These findings suggest significant renal impairment, possible nephrotic syndrome, and tubular dysfunction. The low HbA1c with glucosuria and ketonuria may indicate acute metabolic derangement rather than chronic hyperglycemia. Urinary findings also raise suspicion for possible hepatic involvement or hemolysis. Prompt clinical evaluation is recommended.

display.value: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
